instruction: Demonstrate Chrome DevTools mastery for debugging JavaScript: advanced breakpoint types, performance profiling, memory leak detection with heap snapshots, and debugging async code with the async call stack.
output:
<think>
Chrome DevTools is the most powerful JavaScript debugging environment. Let me cover the advanced features that most developers don't use.

Advanced breakpoint types:
1. Conditional breakpoints: only break when expression is true
2. Logpoints: log without pausing (like console.log but no code change)
3. DOM breakpoints: break on DOM mutations
4. XHR/Fetch breakpoints: break on specific network requests
5. Event listener breakpoints: break on specific events
6. Exception breakpoints: break on caught/uncaught exceptions

Performance profiling:
- Performance tab: flame chart, call tree, bottom-up analysis
- Markers: performance.mark() and performance.measure()
- Long task detection
- Layout thrashing identification

Memory debugging:
- Heap snapshots: find what's retaining memory
- Allocation timeline: see when allocations happen
- Comparison view: find growing objects between snapshots
- Retainer chains: trace back to the root keeping objects alive

Async debugging:
- Async stack traces: see the full async call chain
- Promise rejection tracking
- setTimeout/setInterval tracking

I'll write practical JavaScript code that exercises these features, with inline comments explaining which DevTools feature to use.
</think>

```javascript
// ===== Performance Monitoring API =====

class PerformanceTracker {
  constructor() {
    this.markers = new Map();
  }

  // Use with DevTools Performance tab -> User Timing section
  startMark(name) {
    performance.mark(`${name}-start`);
    this.markers.set(name, performance.now());
  }

  endMark(name) {
    performance.mark(`${name}-end`);
    performance.measure(name, `${name}-start`, `${name}-end`);
    const duration = performance.now() - this.markers.get(name);
    console.log(`[perf] ${name}: ${duration.toFixed(2)}ms`);
    return duration;
  }

  // Detect long tasks (>50ms) using PerformanceObserver
  observeLongTasks() {
    if (typeof PerformanceObserver !== 'undefined') {
      const observer = new PerformanceObserver((list) => {
        for (const entry of list.getEntries()) {
          console.warn(
            `[LONG TASK] ${entry.duration.toFixed(0)}ms at ${entry.startTime.toFixed(0)}ms`,
            entry
          );
        }
      });
      try {
        observer.observe({ entryTypes: ['longtask'] });
      } catch (e) {
        // longtask not supported in all environments
      }
      return observer;
    }
  }
}

// ===== Memory Leak Patterns & Detection =====

// LEAK PATTERN 1: Growing event listeners
class LeakyEventManager {
  constructor() {
    this.handlers = []; // never cleaned up
  }

  // Each call adds a closure that captures `data` — never removed
  addHandler(element, data) {
    const handler = () => {
      console.log('Handling with data:', data.length, 'bytes');
    };
    // DevTools: Memory > Heap Snapshot > Search for "LeakyEventManager"
    // Look at the retainer chain to see closures holding `data`
    this.handlers.push(handler);
    // element.addEventListener('click', handler);
  }
}

// FIXED: WeakRef-based or explicit cleanup
class CleanEventManager {
  constructor() {
    this.handlers = new Map();
  }

  addHandler(element, id, handler) {
    this.handlers.set(id, { element, handler });
    // element.addEventListener('click', handler);
  }

  removeHandler(id) {
    const entry = this.handlers.get(id);
    if (entry) {
      // entry.element.removeEventListener('click', entry.handler);
      this.handlers.delete(id);
    }
  }

  cleanup() {
    for (const [id] of this.handlers) {
      this.removeHandler(id);
    }
  }
}

// LEAK PATTERN 2: Closures capturing large objects
function createLeakyClosure() {
  const largeData = new Array(100000).fill('x').join(''); // ~100KB

  // This returned function captures `largeData` even if it never uses it!
  // DevTools: Heap snapshot > Find "system / Closure" > Check retainers
  return function smallFunction() {
    return 42; // doesn't use largeData, but closure still retains it
  };
}

// FIXED: Don't capture unnecessary variables
function createCleanClosure() {
  const largeData = new Array(100000).fill('x').join('');
  const result = largeData.length; // extract what we need
  // largeData is now eligible for GC
  return function smallFunction() {
    return result;
  };
}

// ===== Async Debugging Helpers =====

// DevTools: Sources > Call Stack panel shows async frames
// Enable: DevTools Settings > "Async stack traces"

async function fetchWithRetry(url, maxRetries = 3) {
  // DevTools: Set conditional breakpoint here
  // Condition: retries > 1
  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {
    try {
      // DevTools: XHR/Fetch breakpoint on URL pattern
      const response = await fetch(url);
      if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`);
      return await response.json();
    } catch (error) {
      console.warn(`Attempt ${attempt}/${maxRetries} failed:`, error.message);
      if (attempt === maxRetries) throw error;
      // DevTools: Logpoint here: `Retrying in ${1000 * attempt}ms`
      await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 1000 * attempt));
    }
  }
}

// Promise rejection tracking
function setupUnhandledRejectionTracking() {
  // DevTools: Sources > Breakpoints > "Pause on exceptions" > include caught
  if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection', (event) => {
      console.error(
        '[Unhandled Rejection]',
        event.reason,
        '\nPromise:', event.promise
      );
      // DevTools will show the async stack trace here
    });
  }

  if (typeof process !== 'undefined') {
    process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, promise) => {
      console.error('[Unhandled Rejection]', reason);
    });
  }
}

// ===== Layout Thrashing Detection =====

function detectLayoutThrashing() {
  // BAD: Forces layout between each read/write
  // DevTools: Performance tab > look for purple "Layout" blocks
  const elements = document.querySelectorAll?.('.item') || [];

  // Bad pattern (layout thrashing):
  // for (const el of elements) {
  //   const height = el.offsetHeight;  // FORCE LAYOUT (read)
  //   el.style.height = height + 10 + 'px'; // INVALIDATE (write)
  // }

  // Good pattern (batch reads, then batch writes):
  const heights = Array.from(elements).map((el) => el.offsetHeight); // batch read
  elements.forEach((el, i) => {
    el.style.height = heights[i] + 10 + 'px'; // batch write
  });
}

// ===== Demo: DevTools Commands Reference =====

console.log('%c=== Chrome DevTools Advanced Reference ===', 'font-weight: bold; font-size: 14px');

console.log(`
Breakpoints:
  Right-click line number > "Add conditional breakpoint"
  Right-click line number > "Add logpoint"  (logs without pausing)
  Sources > XHR/Fetch Breakpoints > add URL pattern
  Sources > DOM Breakpoints > right-click DOM node > "Break on..."
  Sources > Event Listener Breakpoints > expand category

Memory Analysis:
  Memory tab > "Take heap snapshot"
  Compare two snapshots: Comparison view shows growing objects
  Allocation timeline: Memory tab > "Allocation instrumentation on timeline"
  Find retainers: expand object > "Retainers" panel

Performance:
  Performance tab > Record > interact > Stop
  Look for: red triangles (long tasks), purple blocks (layout)
  Bottom-Up view: find slowest functions
  Call Tree view: find hot paths

Console Utilities:
  $0           — currently selected DOM element
  $x('xpath')  — XPath selector
  copy(obj)    — copy to clipboard
  monitor(fn)  — log all calls to fn
  getEventListeners($0) — show element's listeners
  queryObjects(Constructor) — find all instances
`);

// Run demos
const tracker = new PerformanceTracker();
setupUnhandledRejectionTracking();

tracker.startMark('leak-demo');
const leaky = createLeakyClosure();
const clean = createCleanClosure();
console.log('Leaky result:', leaky(), 'Clean result:', clean());
tracker.endMark('leak-demo');
```

instruction: Implement Node.js debugging techniques using the built-in inspector, performance hooks, and diagnostic channels for debugging production applications without stopping the process.
output:
<think>
Node.js has powerful built-in debugging capabilities that don't require third-party tools.

Key features:
1. `--inspect` flag: Chrome DevTools debugging over WebSocket
2. `process._debugProcess(pid)`: attach debugger to running process
3. `perf_hooks`: high-resolution performance measurement
4. `diagnostics_channel`: zero-cost diagnostic events
5. `async_hooks`: track async resource lifecycle
6. `v8.writeHeapSnapshot()`: on-demand heap dumps
7. `SIGUSR1`: enable inspector on running process

I'll show:
1. Inspector API for programmatic debugging
2. Performance hooks with histogram
3. Diagnostics channels for tracing without overhead
4. Heap snapshot on demand
5. Async hooks for tracking promise chains
6. CPU profiling via inspector
</think>

```javascript
const { performance, PerformanceObserver, createHistogram } = require('perf_hooks');
const { Session } = require('inspector');
const diagnostics_channel = require('diagnostics_channel');
const v8 = require('v8');
const fs = require('fs');

// ===== Performance Monitoring =====

class PerfMonitor {
  constructor() {
    this.histograms = new Map();
    this.marks = new Map();

    // Observe performance entries
    this.observer = new PerformanceObserver((items) => {
      for (const entry of items.getEntries()) {
        if (entry.entryType === 'measure') {
          const h = this.getHistogram(entry.name);
          h.record(Math.round(entry.duration * 1000)); // microseconds
        }
      }
    });
    this.observer.observe({ entryTypes: ['measure', 'function'] });
  }

  getHistogram(name) {
    if (!this.histograms.has(name)) {
      this.histograms.set(name, createHistogram());
    }
    return this.histograms.get(name);
  }

  startTimer(name) {
    performance.mark(`${name}:start`);
  }

  endTimer(name) {
    performance.mark(`${name}:end`);
    performance.measure(name, `${name}:start`, `${name}:end`);
  }

  // Wrap a function with automatic timing
  timedFunction(name, fn) {
    return performance.timerify(fn);
  }

  report() {
    console.log('\n=== Performance Report ===');
    for (const [name, hist] of this.histograms) {
      console.log(`  ${name}:`);
      console.log(`    count:  ${hist.count}`);
      console.log(`    min:    ${(hist.min / 1000).toFixed(2)}ms`);
      console.log(`    max:    ${(hist.max / 1000).toFixed(2)}ms`);
      console.log(`    mean:   ${(hist.mean / 1000).toFixed(2)}ms`);
      console.log(`    p99:    ${(hist.percentile(99) / 1000).toFixed(2)}ms`);
    }
  }
}

// ===== Diagnostics Channels (zero-cost when no subscribers) =====

const httpChannel = diagnostics_channel.channel('app:http');
const dbChannel = diagnostics_channel.channel('app:db');
const errorChannel = diagnostics_channel.channel('app:error');

// Subscribers — only add overhead when subscribed
function enableDiagnostics() {
  diagnostics_channel.subscribe('app:http', (message, name) => {
    console.log(`[diag:http] ${message.method} ${message.path} ${message.status} ${message.duration}ms`);
  });

  diagnostics_channel.subscribe('app:db', (message, name) => {
    console.log(`[diag:db] ${message.query} (${message.duration}ms, ${message.rows} rows)`);
  });

  diagnostics_channel.subscribe('app:error', (message, name) => {
    console.error(`[diag:error] ${message.error} at ${message.location}`);
  });
}

// Publish events (zero cost if no subscribers)
function simulateHttp(method, path) {
  const start = performance.now();
  // ... handle request ...
  const duration = performance.now() - start;
  if (httpChannel.hasSubscribers) {
    httpChannel.publish({ method, path, status: 200, duration: duration.toFixed(2) });
  }
}

function simulateDbQuery(query) {
  const start = performance.now();
  // ... execute query ...
  const duration = performance.now() - start;
  if (dbChannel.hasSubscribers) {
    dbChannel.publish({ query, duration: duration.toFixed(2), rows: 42 });
  }
}

// ===== Inspector Session for CPU Profiling =====

async function cpuProfile(durationMs = 1000) {
  const session = new Session();
  session.connect();

  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    session.post('Profiler.enable', () => {
      session.post('Profiler.start', () => {
        console.log(`[profiler] CPU profiling for ${durationMs}ms...`);

        setTimeout(() => {
          session.post('Profiler.stop', (err, { profile }) => {
            if (err) return reject(err);

            const filename = `/tmp/cpu-${Date.now()}.cpuprofile`;
            fs.writeFileSync(filename, JSON.stringify(profile));
            console.log(`[profiler] CPU profile saved: ${filename}`);
            console.log('  Load in Chrome DevTools > Performance tab');

            session.post('Profiler.disable');
            session.disconnect();
            resolve(filename);
          });
        }, durationMs);
      });
    });
  });
}

// ===== Heap Snapshot on Demand =====

function takeHeapSnapshot() {
  const filename = v8.writeHeapSnapshot();
  console.log(`[heap] Snapshot saved: ${filename}`);
  console.log('  Load in Chrome DevTools > Memory tab');
  return filename;
}

// ===== Signal-based debugging =====

function setupSignalHandlers() {
  // SIGUSR2: Take heap snapshot
  process.on('SIGUSR2', () => {
    console.log('[signal] SIGUSR2 received — taking heap snapshot');
    takeHeapSnapshot();
  });

  // Note: SIGUSR1 is reserved for starting the inspector
  // Send `kill -USR1 <pid>` to enable Chrome DevTools debugging

  console.log(`[signals] PID: ${process.pid}`);
  console.log('  kill -USR1 ' + process.pid + '  # enable Chrome inspector');
  console.log('  kill -USR2 ' + process.pid + '  # take heap snapshot');
}

// ===== Demo =====

async function main() {
  const monitor = new PerfMonitor();
  enableDiagnostics();
  setupSignalHandlers();

  // Simulate work with timing
  for (let i = 0; i < 20; i++) {
    monitor.startTimer('request');

    simulateHttp('GET', '/api/users');
    simulateDbQuery('SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10');

    // Simulate variable-duration work
    const work = () => {
      let sum = 0;
      for (let j = 0; j < 100000 * (1 + Math.random()); j++) sum += j;
      return sum;
    };
    work();

    monitor.endTimer('request');
  }

  monitor.report();

  // CPU profile (short duration for demo)
  // Uncomment to actually profile:
  // await cpuProfile(500);

  console.log('\n=== Node.js Debug Commands ===');
  console.log('  node --inspect app.js           # enable Chrome DevTools');
  console.log('  node --inspect-brk app.js       # break on first line');
  console.log('  node --heapsnapshot-signal=SIGUSR2 app.js  # heap snapshot on signal');
  console.log('  node --diagnostic-dir=/tmp app.js          # diagnostic output dir');
  console.log('  NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=4096" node app.js');
}

main().catch(console.error);
```

instruction: Implement source map debugging techniques for JavaScript: generating source maps, debugging transpiled/bundled code, handling source map errors, and building a source map consumer for production error reporting.
output:
<think>
Source maps are essential for debugging transpiled/minified JavaScript. They map positions in generated code back to original source.

Key concepts:
1. Source map format: JSON with version, sources, mappings (VLQ-encoded)
2. Generation: bundlers (webpack, esbuild) and transpilers (TypeScript, Babel) produce them
3. Consumption: browsers use them for DevTools, error reporting services decode them
4. The `//# sourceMappingURL=` comment links generated code to its map

I'll show:
1. How source maps work internally (the VLQ encoding)
2. Building a simple source map consumer for error decoding
3. Common source map problems and solutions
4. Production error reporting with source map resolution
5. Handling inline source maps (data URLs)

For the consumer, I'll implement the core logic:
- Parse the source map JSON
- Decode VLQ mappings
- Binary search to find the original position for a generated position
- Return original file, line, column, and name
</think>

```javascript
// ===== VLQ Decoding (core of source map format) =====

const VLQ_BASE_SHIFT = 5;
const VLQ_BASE = 1 << VLQ_BASE_SHIFT; // 32
const VLQ_BASE_MASK = VLQ_BASE - 1;   // 31
const VLQ_CONTINUATION = VLQ_BASE;     // 32

const BASE64_CHARS = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/';
const base64Decode = new Map([...BASE64_CHARS].map((c, i) => [c, i]));

function decodeVLQ(encoded) {
  const values = [];
  let i = 0;

  while (i < encoded.length) {
    let value = 0;
    let shift = 0;
    let digit;

    do {
      digit = base64Decode.get(encoded[i++]);
      if (digit === undefined) throw new Error(`Invalid base64: ${encoded[i-1]}`);
      value += (digit & VLQ_BASE_MASK) << shift;
      shift += VLQ_BASE_SHIFT;
    } while (digit & VLQ_CONTINUATION);

    // Sign is stored in the least significant bit
    const isNegative = value & 1;
    value >>= 1;
    values.push(isNegative ? -value : value);
  }

  return values;
}

// ===== Source Map Consumer =====

class SourceMapConsumer {
  constructor(rawSourceMap) {
    const map = typeof rawSourceMap === 'string'
      ? JSON.parse(rawSourceMap)
      : rawSourceMap;

    this.version = map.version;
    this.sources = map.sources || [];
    this.sourcesContent = map.sourcesContent || [];
    this.names = map.names || [];
    this.mappings = this._parseMappings(map.mappings || '');
  }

  _parseMappings(mappingsStr) {
    const mappings = [];
    const lines = mappingsStr.split(';');

    let sourceIndex = 0;
    let sourceLine = 0;
    let sourceColumn = 0;
    let nameIndex = 0;

    for (let genLine = 0; genLine < lines.length; genLine++) {
      const line = lines[genLine];
      if (!line) continue;

      let genColumn = 0;
      const segments = line.split(',');

      for (const segment of segments) {
        if (!segment) continue;
        const fields = decodeVLQ(segment);

        genColumn += fields[0];

        const mapping = {
          generatedLine: genLine + 1,
          generatedColumn: genColumn,
        };

        if (fields.length >= 4) {
          sourceIndex += fields[1];
          sourceLine += fields[2];
          sourceColumn += fields[3];
          mapping.source = this.sources[sourceIndex];
          mapping.originalLine = sourceLine + 1;
          mapping.originalColumn = sourceColumn;
        }

        if (fields.length >= 5) {
          nameIndex += fields[4];
          mapping.name = this.names[nameIndex];
        }

        mappings.push(mapping);
      }
    }

    return mappings;
  }

  /**
   * Find the original position for a generated position.
   * @param {number} line - 1-based line in generated file
   * @param {number} column - 0-based column in generated file
   */
  originalPositionFor(line, column) {
    // Find the closest mapping at or before the given position
    let best = null;

    for (const mapping of this.mappings) {
      if (mapping.generatedLine === line && mapping.generatedColumn <= column) {
        if (!best || mapping.generatedColumn > best.generatedColumn) {
          best = mapping;
        }
      }
    }

    if (!best || !best.source) {
      return { source: null, line: null, column: null, name: null };
    }

    return {
      source: best.source,
      line: best.originalLine,
      column: best.originalColumn,
      name: best.name || null,
    };
  }

  /**
   * Get the original source content for a file.
   */
  sourceContentFor(source) {
    const idx = this.sources.indexOf(source);
    return idx >= 0 ? this.sourcesContent[idx] || null : null;
  }
}

// ===== Production Error Reporter =====

class ErrorReporter {
  constructor() {
    this.sourceMaps = new Map(); // url -> SourceMapConsumer
  }

  loadSourceMap(generatedUrl, sourceMapJson) {
    this.sourceMaps.set(generatedUrl, new SourceMapConsumer(sourceMapJson));
  }

  /**
   * Resolve a stack trace to original positions.
   */
  resolveStackTrace(stackTrace) {
    const frameRegex = /at\s+(?:(.+?)\s+)?\(?(.+?):(\d+):(\d+)\)?/g;
    const resolved = [];

    let match;
    while ((match = frameRegex.exec(stackTrace)) !== null) {
      const [, fnName, file, line, col] = match;
      const consumer = this.sourceMaps.get(file);

      if (consumer) {
        const original = consumer.originalPositionFor(
          parseInt(line, 10),
          parseInt(col, 10)
        );
        resolved.push({
          function: original.name || fnName || '<anonymous>',
          file: original.source || file,
          line: original.line || parseInt(line, 10),
          column: original.column || parseInt(col, 10),
          mapped: !!original.source,
        });
      } else {
        resolved.push({
          function: fnName || '<anonymous>',
          file,
          line: parseInt(line, 10),
          column: parseInt(col, 10),
          mapped: false,
        });
      }
    }

    return resolved;
  }

  formatStack(frames) {
    return frames
      .map((f) => `  at ${f.function} (${f.file}:${f.line}:${f.column})${f.mapped ? ' [mapped]' : ''}`)
      .join('\n');
  }
}

// ===== Demo =====

// Simulated source map (simplified)
const exampleSourceMap = {
  version: 3,
  file: 'bundle.js',
  sources: ['src/app.ts', 'src/utils.ts'],
  sourcesContent: [
    'function main() {\n  const x = getValue();\n  console.log(x);\n}',
    'export function getValue(): number {\n  return 42;\n}',
  ],
  names: ['main', 'getValue', 'console', 'log'],
  mappings: 'AAAA,SAASA,IACPC,MAAMC,EAAIC,IACbC,QAAQC,IAAIF',
};

const consumer = new SourceMapConsumer(exampleSourceMap);

console.log('=== Source Map Consumer ===');
const pos = consumer.originalPositionFor(1, 0);
console.log('Generated 1:0 ->', pos);

// Error reporter
const reporter = new ErrorReporter();
reporter.loadSourceMap('bundle.js', exampleSourceMap);

const fakeStack = `Error: Something went wrong
    at processData (bundle.js:1:15)
    at handleRequest (bundle.js:1:45)
    at main (bundle.js:1:0)`;

console.log('\n=== Resolved Stack Trace ===');
const frames = reporter.resolveStackTrace(fakeStack);
console.log(reporter.formatStack(frames));

console.log('\n=== Source Map Tips ===');
console.log('1. Always generate source maps in production builds');
console.log('2. Upload maps to error tracking (Sentry, etc.), dont serve publicly');
console.log('3. Use hidden-source-map webpack devtool for production');
console.log('4. Include sourcesContent for standalone debugging');
console.log('5. Validate maps: npx sourcemap-validator bundle.js bundle.js.map');
```
